yearMetaText: When Zunftmeister Conrad Bavier opened the inherited family house as an inn, with the shield of the three kings.

bodyParagraphs.text: The hotel has existed in this building since 1793. Recorded in the National Archives: Mr. Zunftmeister Conrad Bavier established the inherited house as an inn, and gave it the shield with the three kings. The Schällibaum family ran the house for over three generations, until we bought it in 2005. Since then, we have continued to operate it in the same building, in the same old town, with the same name above the door.

Inside the walls there are details most guests never notice: the oldest preserved hotel room in Chur, a banquet hall that has been alternately part of an abbey and part of the seat of government, and — according to local legend — an underground passage that once connected the bishop's seat to our basement.

quote: "Established the inherited house as an inn, and gave it the shield with the three kings."
